summary: Frontend-only validation of the new Rufzeichen (callsign) module + Morse/Antenna regressions on Expo web preview (mobile viewport 375x667). All 7 requested flows pass: callsign tile is ACTIVE (opacity 1, no lock badge) and navigates to the Rufzeichen screen (header 'Rufzeichen' visible in screenshot); callsign-input auto-uppercases 'dl5xyz' -> 'DL5XYZ'; callsign-search-button is disabled when empty (aria-disabled=true, opacity 0.5) and enabled once text is entered (opacity 1); pressing Suchen renders the German testID='callsign-web-notice' with the expected copy ('Die Live-Suche funktioniert nur in der mobilen App ...'), which per the agent-to-agent context is the correct web-preview behaviour; header-back-button returns to hub. Regressions: Morse tile opens the Morse screen; Antenna forward mode with freq=14.200 + lambda-chip-1-2 yields Drahtlänge '10,04 m'; reverse mode with antenna-length-input=20.10 renders antenna-bands-list with 80/40/20/15/10 m entries.
critical_code_review_comments: callsign.tsx is well-scoped (~340 lines); native-only WebView paths are correctly gated on Platform.OS !== 'web' and the extraction/fallback JS is injected via injectedJavaScript which cannot be validated in the web preview (documented and expected).
TextInput sanitizer (t.toUpperCase().replace(/[^A-Z0-9/]/g, '')) correctly restricts to A-Z/0-9/ and enforces uppercase — verified live.
Search button uses Pressable disabled + opacity 0.5 (aria-disabled surfaces on web) — accessible disabled state confirmed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Live HamQTH lookup uses react-native-webview and is NATIVE-ONLY; the web preview correctly shows testID='callsign-web-notice' after pressing Suchen — do NOT treat the absence of Name/QTH result rows as a bug in the web preview. Verify Name/QTH/Country/Grid/CQ/ITU extraction only on Expo Go / native build.
